     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→Provides Git forge provider integration for automated merge request creation and management in transflow workflows. Currently implements GitLab REST API with GitHub support infrastructure ready.
     5→
     6→## Narrative Summary
     7→The git provider module implements a clean interface for interacting with Git forge providers (currently GitLab, extensible to GitHub). It handles authentication, project URL parsing, merge request lifecycle management, and comprehensive error handling. The module supports both creating new merge requests and updating existing ones based on source branch matching.
     8→
     9→The provider follows the principle of graceful degradation - MR creation failures do not break the parent workflow, allowing transformations to complete even when Git forge integration is unavailable. It includes comprehensive mock implementations for testing and CI/CD workflows.
    10→
    11→## Key Files
    12→- `interface.go:5-26` - GitProvider interface and data structures
    13→- `gitlab.go:14-272` - Complete GitLab REST API implementation
    14→- `gitlab.go:45-80` - Main CreateOrUpdateMR orchestration logic
    15→- `gitlab.go:82-117` - Existing MR detection and conflict resolution
    16→- `gitlab.go:119-180` - MR creation and update API calls
    17→- `gitlab.go:218-272` - GitLab project URL parsing and validation with nested namespace support
    18→- `gitlab_test.go:1-300` - Comprehensive unit tests with mock HTTP responses and edge case handling
    19→
    20→## API Interface
    21→### GitProvider Interface
    22→- `CreateOrUpdateMR(ctx, config) -> (*MRResult, error)` - Primary MR management
    23→- `ValidateConfiguration() -> error` - Pre-flight configuration check
    24→
    25→### Data Structures
    26→- `MRConfig:12-19` - Input parameters for MR creation
    27→- `MRResult:22-26` - Output result with URLs and metadata
    28→
    29→## Integration Points
    30→### Consumes
    31→- GitLab REST API: Projects endpoint (`/api/v4/projects/:id`) and merge requests endpoint (`/api/v4/projects/:id/merge_requests`)
    32→- Environment variables: GITLAB_URL, GITLAB_TOKEN
    33→- HTTP client with proper timeout and error handling
    34→
    35→### Provides
    36→- Merge request creation/updates for transflow workflows with rich MR descriptions
    37→- Project URL validation and parsing supporting nested GitLab namespaces
    38→- Authentication validation with pre-flight token checking
    39→- Mock implementations for comprehensive testing infrastructure
    40→
    41→## Configuration
    42→Required environment variables:
    43→- `GITLAB_TOKEN` - GitLab API token with project access (API scope required)
    44→- `GITLAB_URL` - GitLab instance URL (optional, defaults to https://gitlab.com)
    45→
    46→GitHub support infrastructure (via VPS Ansible setup):
    47→- `GITHUB_PLOY_DEV_USERNAME` - GitHub username for development authentication
    48→- `GITHUB_PLOY_DEV_PAT` - GitHub personal access token for API access
    49→
    50→Supported repository URL formats:
    51→- `https://gitlab.example.com/namespace/project.git`
    52→- `https://gitlab.com/group/subgroup/project.git`
    53→- `https://gitlab.com/deeply/nested/namespace/project.git`
    54→- Automatic `.git` suffix handling and URL normalization
    55→
    56→## Authentication Patterns
    57→- Bearer token authentication via Authorization header
    58→- Token validation on service initialization
    59→- Graceful error handling for invalid/expired tokens
    60→
    61→## Key Patterns
    62→- Interface-based design for provider extensibility (GitProvider interface)
    63→- URL parsing with comprehensive validation supporting nested namespaces (see gitlab.go:218-272)
    64→- Idempotent operations - safe to retry MR creation with branch-based conflict resolution
    65→- Context-aware HTTP requests with proper timeout and cancellation support
    66→- Rich MR descriptions with workflow metadata and transformation summaries
    67→- Graceful error handling with detailed error context for debugging
    68→- Comprehensive mock infrastructure for testing without external dependencies

    12→
    13→// DeployConfig contains all deployment parameters
    14→type DeployConfig struct {
    15→	App           string
    16→	Lane          string
    17→	MainClass     string
    18→	SHA           string
    19→	IsPlatform    bool // true for ployman, false for ploy
    20→	BlueGreen     bool
    21→	Environment   string // dev, staging, prod
    22→	ControllerURL string
    23→	Metadata      map[string]string
    24→	Timeout       time.Duration
    25→}
    26→
    27→// DeployResult contains deployment outcome information
    28→type DeployResult struct {
    29→	Success      bool
    30→	Version      string
    31→	DeploymentID string
    32→	URL          string
    33→	Message      string
    34→}
    35→
    36→// SharedPush handles deployment for both ploy and ployman
    37→func SharedPush(config DeployConfig) (*DeployResult, error) {
    38→	// Validate configuration
    39→	if err := validateConfig(config); err != nil {
    40→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid configuration: %w", err)
    41→	}
    42→
    43→	// Generate SHA if not provided
    44→	if config.SHA == "" {
    45→		if v := utils.GitSHA(); v != "" {
    46→			config.SHA = v
    47→		} else {
    48→			config.SHA = time.Now().Format("20060102-150405")
    49→		}
    50→	}
    51→
    52→	// Create tar archive
    53→	ign, _ := utils.ReadGitignore(".")
    54→	pr, pw := io.Pipe()
    55→	go func() {
    56→		defer pw.Close()
    57→		_ = utils.TarDir(".", pw, ign)
    58→	}()
    59→
    60→	// Build deployment URL
    61→	url := buildDeployURL(config)
    62→
    63→	// Create HTTP request
    64→	req, _ := http.NewRequest("POST", url, pr)
    65→	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-tar")
    66→
    67→	// Add platform-specific headers
    68→	if config.IsPlatform {
    69→		req.Header.Set("X-Platform-Service", "true")
    70→		req.Header.Set("X-Target-Domain", "ployman.app")
    71→	} else {
    72→		req.Header.Set("X-Target-Domain", "ployd.app")
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	// Add environment header
    76→	if config.Environment != "" {
    77→		req.Header.Set("X-Environment", config.Environment)
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	// Execute request with optional timeout
    81→	client := &http.Client{}
    82→	if config.Timeout > 0 {
    83→		client.Timeout = config.Timeout
    84→	}
    85→	resp, err := client.Do(req)
    86→	if err != nil {
    87→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("deployment request failed: %w", err)
    88→	}
    89→	defer resp.Body.Close()
    90→
    91→	// Parse response
    92→	result, err := parseDeployResponse(resp, config)
    93→	if err != nil {
    94→		return nil, err
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	// Output to console
    98→	io.Copy(os.Stdout, resp.Body)
    99→
   100→	return result, nil
   101→}
   102→
   103→// validateConfig validates the deployment configuration
   104→func validateConfig(config DeployConfig) error {
   105→	if config.App == "" {
   106→		return fmt.Errorf("app name is required")
   107→	}
   108→	if config.ControllerURL == "" {
